title: Prologue
content:
A short prologue that hints at what is to come. The protagonist moved through the
unfamiliar landscape with a quiet attentiveness, noting every shift in light and weather as
if reading a language she half understood. The path narrowed as it climbed, then
widened again where a small stream cut across it. She paused at the crossing to drink,
cupping cold water in her hands. The taste was clean and stony, almost mineral. She did
not know yet that the choice of which fork to take would shape the next several days.
The map she carried was old, drawn long before the floods of the previous spring, and
several landmarks had already moved or vanished entirely. By the time she reached the
ridge, the sun had dropped behind the higher peaks and the valley below sat in a soft
blue shadow. Lights began to appear in the windows of the houses scattered across the

title: Departure
content:
She leaves home for the first time, uncertain but determined. The protagonist moved
through the unfamiliar landscape with a quiet attentiveness, noting every shift in light
and weather as if reading a language she half understood. The path narrowed as it

time she reached the ridge, the sun had dropped behind the higher peaks and the valley
below sat in a soft blue shadow. Lights began to appear in the windows of the houses

content:

title: Return
content:

title: Epilogue
content:
Years later, the events of that summer are remembered differently. The protagonist
moved through the unfamiliar landscape with a quiet attentiveness, noting every shift in
light and weather as if reading a language she half understood. The path narrowed as it
